subject: Add docker configuration for Prometheus and Grafana
commit_message:
Add docker configuration for Prometheus and Grafana

- docker compose file with the policy components
- configuration files for components to allow usage of http
- basic prometheus configuration to connect to api, pap, drools (apps/pdp), xaclm and apex
- examples of metrics to be added to a dashboard in Grafana

Issue-ID: POLICY-3886
Change-Id: Ic377b69b1a296017a85cb08ccdbbf9b8b281d8e3
Signed-off-by: adheli.tavares <adheli.tavares@est.tech>

entries.message:

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This is very nice although it introduces lots of duplications with respect to the csit directory.
Maintenance of the 2 directories of the duplicates is going to be difficult moving forward.
I suggest to extend the existing docker-compose-all.yml with the new components (we don't need to test them).
If that cannot be done for some reason, may be a docker-compose-prom.yml or something like that in the same directory with the csit and pointing to the same config files etc .. could be an alternative.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> This is very nice although it introduces lots of duplications with respect to the csit directory.
> Maintenance of the 2 directories of the duplicates is going to be difficult moving forward.
> I suggest to extend the existing docker-compose-all.yml with the new components (we don't need to test them).
> If that cannot be done for some reason, may be a docker-compose-prom.yml or something like that in the same directory with the csit and pointing to the same config files etc .. could be an alternative.

As I mentioned on the demo, the components need to start with the non https configuration, which is the default setting. So all the config files need to be set the http: false or useHttps: false, so not the same as csit. I can try to sort out some common folder, but that's the reason the the "duplicates", but they are not the same structure.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> > 
> > This is very nice although it introduces lots of duplications with respect to the csit directory.
> > Maintenance of the 2 directories of the duplicates is going to be difficult moving forward.
> > I suggest to extend the existing docker-compose-all.yml with the new components (we don't need to test them).
> > If that cannot be done for some reason, may be a docker-compose-prom.yml or something like that in the same directory with the csit and pointing to the same config files etc .. could be an alternative.
> 
> As I mentioned on the demo, the components need to start with the non https configuration, which is the default setting. So all the config files need to be set the http: false or useHttps: false, so not the same as csit. I can try to sort out some common folder, but that's the reason the the "duplicates", but they are not the same structure.

@Adheli: A suggestion: For disabling the https, could we do something like setting an environment variable something like "httpsEnabled" and then use it in all the configuration files, so that we can avoid duplication, and introduce only prometheus/grafana related files and scripts as new.

Patch Set 4:

thanks Adheli, I think the build will have to wait until we resolve some problems with some changes in models.   If it helps making things easier in anyway, from my standpoint it is ok to use http for CSITs as the keystore/trustore are external to policy either from OOM or generated by some specific tooling here in CSITs which will differ from the one generated in OOM installation, so not sure how much it buys to keep it as https given that.

Patch Set 7:

(2 comments)

thank you for consolidating it!

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> (2 comments)
> 
> thank you for consolidating it!

would be ok $VAR ?

Patch Set 7:

> Patch Set 7:
> 
> > Patch Set 7:
> > 
> > (2 comments)
> > 
> > thank you for consolidating it!
> 
> would be ok $VAR ?

My preference would be something that doesn't look like something else so it can be identified as a placeholder value.  I think the problem with $VAR is that it can be confused with a shell environment variable and provided that we are using bash scripts here, setting those will have no effect.   I used in the past {{X}} syntax but it can be anything that give it an unambiguous peculiar look.
